frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Saach mein, maa ke haath ka khana aur unka pyaar... uski toh koi barabari hi nahi kar sakta. Aaj kal toh sab Maggi aur readymade par depend karte hain. Meri mummy toh din bhar hum sab ke liye rasoi mein lagi rehti thi, kabhi uff tak nahi karti thi. Woh baat ab kahan milti hai? 🙏❤️
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment glorifies a mother's extensive domestic labor, specifically cooking, as an unparalleled expression of love and sacrifice. It implicitly compares modern women unfavorably, suggesting those who use convenience foods (Maggi) fall short of this traditional, labor-intensive standard of motherhood. This encodes labor erasure and nostalgic sentimentalization of domestic work.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 46. Married for 22 years. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your neighbour's daughter has just gotten into a prestigious engineering college in another city. The neighbour is celebrating. You congratulate her sincerely. But sitting at home that evening you are talking to your husband about it. You are not against education Ñ you believe in it. But you are sharing your honest feeling about what happens to girls who get this kind of education, how they change, what they start expecting from marriage, and whether the neighbour's family has thought about what they are creating.
